Industry data confirms that high-performing organizations aren’t using software to replace staff, but to build high-velocity human-in-the-loop operational gates.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Summary
Recent mid-year 2026 data from Gartner and KPMG reveals a widening gap between organizations making massive investments in artificial intelligence and those actually realizing measurable financial returns. While spending on advanced tools continues to accelerate across all business sizes, the vast majority of deployments stall because they rely on the flawed assumption that software can run independently to cut labor costs. High-performing organizations, by contrast, are achieving up to three times the returns by deploying a fundamentally different architecture: the Amplification Index. Rather than pursuing hands-free automation, these market leaders build tight, human-in-the-loop oversight gates that use software as an instrument to multiply expert output. By recognizing that algorithms lack the contextual judgment to handle unmapped edge cases, this approach turns human supervision into an active engine for velocity, proving that long-term value is unlocked only when human pilots remain anchored at the center of the workflow.
